title: Gerontology Support

title: Sprouted Seeds

narrative: The user seeks detailed, practical guidance on germinating delicate mucilaginous seeds—particularly cressonnette (meadow cress)—including optimal techniques, equipment (e.g., germination cups vs. jars), timing, storage, and culinary uses. Relevance is determined by content that provides step-by-step sprouting instructions, addresses common pitfalls (e.g., mucilage clogging jars), and integrates the sprouts into recipes or nutritional contexts. Documents focused on non-mucilaginous seeds, general sprouting, or unrelated recipes (e.g., salted Christmas sables) are not relevant.

title: Ohlone Canoe Journey

narrative: The user seeks information on cultural revitalization efforts by Ohlone Native Americans who are constructing traditional tule reed canoes to participate in a symbolic voyage to Alcatraz Island. Relevant documents must detail the historical context of the 1969–1971 occupation, the traditional craftsmanship involved in building the canoes from tule reeds, the participation of Ohlone community members like Antonio Moreno, and the cultural, spiritual, and political significance of the journey. Documents focusing on modern ferry services, unrelated French anchoring laws, or non-Native maritime regulations are not relevant.
